div.h1: ODR proceeding stages you can orchestrate digitally
div.p: From intake to interim relief to final outcome, disciplined case management parallels institutional rules published globally — here translated for NBFC portfolios, enterprise disputes, and court-referred ADR programs.

Know-your-party checks, claimant onboarding, escrow of supporting debt instruments, tagging related corporate guarantors — BharatNyay’s vault mirrors best practice registries advocated by arbitration institutions.
Deadlines for statements of claim/defence, document production, witness lists, experts — tribunal secretariat dashboards keep defaults visible to both sides (equality of arms shorthand).
Emergency arbitrators / tribunal powers analogous to Sections 9 & 17 interplay — tooling records requests, security for costs postings, conservatory attachments where applicable.
Bandwidth tests, oath administration, contempt-safe recording policies, interpreters — BharatNyay’s hearing module aligns with UNCITRAL notes on procedural integrity online.
Advance on costs, arbitrator fee mandates, victorious party allocations — surfaced for transparency mandated by tribunal rulesbooks.
Mediated settlements can convert into consent awards ; partial settlements bifurcate issues cleanly before final pleadings conclude.
This lifecycle can run under online mediation, online conciliation, online negotiation, or full arbitration tracks depending on clause design and party consent.

div.span: Pair this briefing with BharatNyay’s workflow screens.
